message:
chore(tracking): sync beads issue tracker with security hardening progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -648,7 +648,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-30lxu","title":"[dgis_migration_gate] Resolve dead-code MAX_EVENTS lint in migration gate","description":"Why this exists:
`cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -- -D warnings` is currently blocked, in part, by a dead-code lint in `crates/franken-node/src/migration/dgis_migration_gate.rs`: the file declares `const MAX_EVENTS: usize = 4096;` and never uses it.

What this task must do:
- inspect the migration gate event path and decide whether the right fix is to remove the unused constant or actually enforce the intended event cap
- keep the change file-local to `crates/franken-node/src/migration/dgis_migration_gate.rs`
- validate with targeted `rch` cargo-heavy checks only

Acceptance criteria:
1. The dead-code lint for `MAX_EVENTS` in `dgis_migration_gate.rs` is eliminated without widening scope beyond this file.
2. Behavior remains deterministic; if an event cap is intended, tests prove it.
3. Cargo-heavy validation uses `rch` only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T20:56:22.965964050Z","created_by":"BronzeCliff","updated_at":"2026-03-12T21:03:36.332625694Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T21:03:36.283818384Z","close_reason":"Removed dead MAX_EVENTS constant; targeted rch clippy+test green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-30lxu","author":"BronzeCliff","text":"Implemented on 2026-03-12:

- Removed the dead `MAX_EVENTS` constant from `crates/franken-node/src/migration/dgis_migration_gate.rs`. The gate only emits a tiny bounded event set plus at most three replan suggestions, so the correct fix was to remove unused dead code rather than invent a fake cap.
- File-local hygiene:
  - rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/migration/dgis_migration_gate.rs -> PASS
  - git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/migration/dgis_migration_gate.rs -> PASS
- Targeted rch validation:
  - cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --test dgis_migration_gate -- -D warnings -> PASS
  - cargo test -p frankenengine-node --test dgis_migration_gate -- --nocapture -> PASS (22 passed, 0 failed)

Result: the `MAX_EVENTS` dead-code lint is eliminated without widening scope beyond `dgis_migration_gate.rs`.","created_at":"2026-03-12T21:03:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3110","title":"Remaining medium-severity audit findings: .expect() in production, lockstep I/O error suppression","description":"Medium-severity findings from deep audit (bd-1sw8 follow-on):

1. cancellable_task.rs: 12 .expect() calls on HashMap lookups in core task lifecycle module
2. vef_degraded_mode.rs:571: .expect() on state machine invariant in degraded-mode handler
3. rollback_bundle.rs:225,251: .expect() on serde_json::to_vec in public API methods
4. lockstep_harness.rs:181,187: let _ = on stdout/stderr read_to_end (silent I/O error)

Lower priority: trust_card.rs HMAC domain separator (mitigated), epoch_scoped_keys.rs HMAC (mitigated by HKDF).
All HIGH/MEDIUM ct_eq, boundary, and domain separator findings were fixed in bd-1sw8.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyLake","created_at":"2026-03-03T03:05:40.104817173Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T04:07:46.928607164Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T04:07:46.928499474Z","close_reason":"Validated on current head: production findings remediated; remaining .expect() usage is test-only","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":33,"issue_id":"bd-3110","author":"SunnyBridge","text":"Read-only spot-check on current head for `bd-3110` scope (no edits made):

- `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lockstep_harness.rs`: current implementation already handles `read_to_end` errors with `if let Err(e)` + stderr logging; I did not find `let _ = read_to_end(...)` suppression in this file now.
- `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/cancellable_task.rs`: `rg '\.expect\('` returns no matches.
- `crates/franken-node/src/connector/rollback_bundle.rs`: remaining `.expect(...)` calls currently appear in test/fixture sections (~line 858+) while public API methods near earlier concern points (around 225/251) are already `Result`-returning.
- `crates/franken-node/src/security/vef_degraded_mode.rs`: remaining `.expect(...)` matches observed in test region (~991+).

If this bead’s acceptance target is production code only (not test helper expectations), portions of the originally reported findings appear already remediated on current head and may need confirmation + scope clarification.
","created_at":"2026-03-03T03:40:24Z"},{"id":35,"issue_id":"bd-3110","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Validation pass on current head (no edits): lockstep_harness read_to_end now logs errors via if let Err(e); cancellable_task has no .expect(); rollback_bundle and vef_degraded_mode .expect() matches are inside #[cfg(test)] sections (cfg(test) starts at rollback_bundle.rs:830 and vef_degraded_mode.rs:627). Requested Agent Mail reservations to patch if needed but conflicts indicate DustyLake currently holds exclusive reservations on these files, so I avoided overlap.","created_at":"2026-03-03T03:47:04Z"}]}

An adversary with valid signatures could exhaust memory by verifying many unique-nonce proofs. Fixed by adding a VecDeque-backed FIFO eviction queue (MAX_CONSUMED_NONCES=65536), matching the pattern used in audience_token.rs and control_channel.rs.  {"id":"bd-31l3","title":"Exploratory deep code audit with targeted bug fixes","description":"Randomized deep investigation across core Rust modules and related call/import flows to find obvious bugs/silly mistakes, implement high-confidence fixes, and validate via required checks (using rch for cargo workloads).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyDune","created_at":"2026-02-22T18:06:46.507779730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:30:24.613434157Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:30:24.613407458Z","close_reason":"Completed: exploratory audit landed targeted fixes in interface_hash and optimization_governor with regression tests and rch verification evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","bugfix","rust"]}
 {"id":"bd-31tg","title":"[15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 15

Task Objective:
Implement partner/lighthouse adoption programs proving category-shift outcomes.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_15/bd-31tg/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_15/bd-31tg/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[15] Pillar: partner and lighthouse programs" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partner program defined with tiers: (a) Lighthouse (early adopters, deep integration, direct support channel), (b) Partner (validated migration, co-marketing), (c) Community (self-service, public resources).
2. Lighthouse program has >= 3 active participants running franken_node in production or staging.
3. Each lighthouse partner has: (a) documented use case, (b) migration case study (published or in-progress), (c) feedback channel with response SLA <= 48 hours, (d) quarterly review cadence.
4. Partner program has clear entry criteria: minimum project size, commitment to provide feedback, willingness to publish anonymized results.
5. Benefits documented: priority bug fixes, early access to features, co-marketing opportunities, benchmark co-design input.
6. Program produces >= 2 published case studies within first 6 months.
7. Partner satisfaction tracked via quarterly survey; target NPS >= 30.
8. Evidence: partner_program_status.json with partner count by tier, case study list, and satisfaction scores.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:36.527116261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T06:41:40.978282557Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T06:41:40.978258742Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31tg","depends_on_id":"bd-1961","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:26.385358774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-36wa","title":"[11] Contract field: compatibility and threat evidence","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 11

Task Objective:
Require compatibility and threat evidence payloads for each major subsystem proposal.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [11] Contract field: compatibility and threat evidence are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [11] Contract field: compatibility and threat evidence are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-36wa/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-36wa/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[11] Contract field: compatibility and threat evidence" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[11] Contract field: compatibility and threat evidence" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every evidence contract includes a compatibility-and-threat-evidence section with: (a) list of compatibility test suites exercised and pass/fail counts, (b) threat model delta — new attack surfaces introduced or closed, (c) regression risk assessment citing specific API families.
2. Compatibility evidence must reference actual test run artifact paths (verification_evidence.json) not just claims.
3. Threat evidence must enumerate at least: privilege escalation, data exfiltration, and denial-of-service vectors with mitigations.
4. CI validation rejects contracts where compatibility evidence references zero test suites or threat section is empty.
5. Unit test: contract with full evidence passes; contract missing threat model or test references fails.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonFinch","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:32.564264525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:11:41.902537011Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:11:41.902493290Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-11","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36wa","depends_on_id":"bd-3se1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:24.327391786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-cda","title":"[PLAN 10.N] Execution Normalization Contract (No Duplicate Implementations)","description":"Section: 10.N — Execution Normalization Contract (No Duplicate Implementations)

Strategic Context:
Execution normalization to prevent duplicate implementations, enforce canonical ownership, and keep cross-track integration coherent.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.N] Execution Normalization Contract (No Duplicate Implementations)" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:40.132488047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T08:41:50.891275800Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T08:41:50.891190381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-N"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cda","depends_on_id":"bd-1neb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:27.220550663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cda","depends_on_id":"bd-1oyt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:50:04.664351830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cda","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:50:04.758779969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cda","depends_on_id":"bd-2yhs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:50:04.569309366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cda","depends_on_id":"bd-zxk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:50:04.448172801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cewu","title":"franken_engine hook_effect_contract uses removed SchemaId::ZERO and old derive_id signature","description":"franken_node validation is blocked by path dependency compile failures in /data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/hook_effect_contract.rs: uses SchemaId::ZERO (removed) and passes integer 0 to derive_id where derive_id now expects zone: &str. Patch call sites to new API and re-run rch validation from franken_node.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OliveDog","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:30:35.548662059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T20:33:47.057431264Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T20:33:47.057409303Z","close_reason":"No longer reproducible at current head; already resolved by concurrent upstream franken_engine edits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-cewu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Created from earlier rch compile output, but local source in /data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/hook_effect_contract.rs now already uses derive_id(ObjectDomain::EvidenceRecord, "hook-effect", &contract_schema(), ...); no SchemaId::ZERO or integer-zone callsites remain. Treating this as already-resolved-by-concurrent-work and not pursuing duplicate edits.","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:33:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ck2h","title":"[10.13] Define MVP vs Full conformance profile matrix and publication claim rules.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Why This Exists:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Task Objective:
Define MVP vs Full conformance profile matrix and publication claim rules.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Profile matrix maps required capabilities to claim language; publication metadata is generated from measured profile results; unsupported claims are blocked.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/conformance/profile_matrix.md`, `tests/conformance/profile_claim_gate.rs`, `artifacts/10.13/profile_claim_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-ck2h/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_13/bd-ck2h/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.13] Define MVP vs Full conformance profile matrix and publication claim rules.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.13] Define MVP vs Full conformance profile matrix and publication claim rules." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.13] Define MVP vs Full conformance profile matrix and publication claim rules." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.13] Define MVP vs Full conformance profile matrix and publication claim rules." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.13] Define MVP vs Full conformance profile matrix and publication claim rules." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.

 {"id":"bd-cuut","title":"[10.15] Define lane mapping policy for control-plane workloads (Cancel/Timed/Ready).","description":"## Why This Exists
Hard Runtime Invariant #5 from Section 8.5 requires scheduler lane discipline: every control-plane task must be assigned to a lane (Cancel, Timed, or Ready) with an explicit priority and budget policy, and starvation across lanes must be detected and prevented. Without lane discipline, a flood of Ready-lane background tasks can starve Cancel-lane shutdown handlers, causing cancellation timeouts and quiescence violations. This bead defines the lane mapping policy for all control-plane workload classes in franken_node's product layer, building on the canonical scheduler lane infrastructure established in Section 10.14 (bd-qlc6).

## What This Must Do
1. Author `docs/specs/control_lane_mapping.md` defining:
   - The three lane classes: **Cancel** (cancellation handlers, drain operations, region close — highest priority, guaranteed minimum budget), **Timed** (health checks, lease renewals, epoch transitions — deadline-bound with timeout enforcement), **Ready** (background maintenance, telemetry flush, evidence archival — best-effort with starvation floor).
   - Per-task-class lane assignments: map every control-plane task type from the workflow inventory (bd-2177) to its lane.
   - Budget policies: Cancel lane gets at least 20% of scheduler capacity; Timed lane gets at least 30%; Ready lane gets remainder.
   - Starvation detection thresholds: if any lane receives zero scheduling slots for N consecutive ticks, emit a starvation alert.
2. Implement `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs` that:
   - Asserts every task class in the control-plane module has a lane assignment in the policy document.
   - Validates budget allocations sum correctly.
   - Simulates a workload mix and asserts no lane is starved (receives zero slots for more than the threshold).
   - Validates that Cancel-lane tasks are scheduled before Ready-lane tasks when both are pending.
3. Generate `artifacts/10.15/lane_starvation_metrics.csv` with columns: `tick, cancel_lane_tasks_run, timed_lane_tasks_run, ready_lane_tasks_run, cancel_lane_starved, timed_lane_starved, ready_lane_starved`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every control task class has lane assignment and budget policy; starvation checks are automated.
- Cancel-lane tasks are never starved for more than 1 tick (immediate priority).
- Budget allocations are enforced in conformance tests with synthetic workloads.
- Lane assignments are machine-readable and consumed by the scheduler integration (from 10.14's bd-qlc6).
- Starvation metrics CSV is consumed by observability dashboards (bd-3gnh).

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- **Unit tests**: Validate lane assignment lookup, budget arithmetic, and starvation threshold detection with synthetic task queues.
- **Integration tests**: Run a mixed workload (cancel + timed + ready tasks) through the scheduler and assert lane discipline.
- **Conformance tests**: Flood the Ready lane and assert Cancel-lane tasks still execute within their budget.
- **Adversarial tests**: Submit cancel-lane tasks that exceed their budget; assert they are preempted. Submit a workload that would starve the Timed lane; assert starvation alert fires.
- **Structured logs**: Event codes `LAN-001` (task assigned to lane), `LAN-002` (lane budget enforced), `LAN-003` (starvation detected), `LAN-004` (starvation resolved), `LAN-005` (task preempted for lane budget). Include task_id, lane_class, budget_remaining_ms, and trace correlation ID.

## Expected Artifacts
- `docs/specs/control_lane_mapping.md`
- `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs`
- `artifacts/10.15/lane_starvation_metrics.csv`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-cuut/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_15/bd-cuut/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies
- **Upstream**: bd-qlc6 (10.14 — canonical scheduler lane infrastructure that this policy builds on)
- **Downstream**: bd-20eg (section gate), bd-lus (10.11 adopts lane policies for product operations)","acceptance_criteria":"- Every control task class has lane assignment and budget policy; starvation checks are automated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:00.053329409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:59:58.378429176Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:59:58.378401494Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cuut","depends_on_id":"bd-qlc6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T14:59:48.249813942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cv49","title":"[15] Adoption target: published security/ops improvement case studies","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 15

Task Objective:
Deliver deterministic migration validation and publish measurable security/ops case studies.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [15] Adoption target: published security/ops improvement case studies are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [15] Adoption target: published security/ops improvement case studies are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_15/bd-cv49/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_15/bd-cv49/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[15] Adoption target: published security/ops improvement case studies" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[15] Adoption target: published security/ops improvement case studies" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At least 3 case studies published documenting real-world security and operational improvements from adopting franken_node.
2. Each case study includes: (a) organization context (size, industry, anonymized if needed), (b) pre-adoption security posture metrics, (c) post-adoption security posture metrics, (d) operational improvements (incident response time, deployment frequency, etc.), (e) migration effort and timeline, (f) lessons learned and recommendations.
3. Quantitative improvements documented: at least 2 case studies show measurable security improvement (e.g., X% reduction in vulnerabilities, Y% faster incident containment).
4. Case studies are reviewed by the featured organization before publication.
5. Case studies are published on project website and submitted to at least 1 industry publication or conference.
6. Case study template exists for partners to self-author with editorial support.
7. Evidence: case_study_registry.json with per-study: title, organization type, key metrics, publication status, and URL.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"NavyPeak","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:36.696204017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:05:06.917484491Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:05:06.917448965Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-15","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cv49","depends_on_id":"bd-elog","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:26.472641055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-cvt","title":"[10.11] Define capability profiles for product subsystems and enforce narrowing.","description":"[10.11] Define capability profiles for product subsystems and enforce narrowing.

## Why This Exists

Section 9G.1 requires least-privilege capability narrowing for every product subsystem. Today, subsystems implicitly have access to all host capabilities (network, filesystem, process spawning, crypto operations, trust-state mutation). This violates the Impossible-by-Default security posture: a compromised or buggy subsystem can escalate beyond its intended scope. This bead establishes explicit capability profiles — each subsystem declares what it needs, and the runtime enforces that declaration. Undeclared capability usage is rejected at compile time (via static analysis) and at runtime (via capability guards).

## What It Must Do

1. **Capability taxonomy.** Define the canonical set of capabilities: `cap:network:listen`, `cap:network:connect`, `cap:fs:read`, `cap:fs:write`, `cap:fs:temp`, `cap:process:spawn`, `cap:crypto:sign`, `cap:crypto:verify`, `cap:crypto:derive`, `cap:trust:read`, `cap:trust:write`, `cap:trust:revoke`. Each capability has a description, risk level (low/medium/high/critical), and audit requirements.

2. **Capability profile declaration.** Each subsystem declares its capability profile in a TOML file (`capabilities/<subsystem>.toml`). The profile lists required capabilities with justification strings. Example: `[capabilities] network_connect = { required = true, justification = "Connects to upstream trust anchors" }`.

3. **Narrowing enforcement at CI.** A CI gate script (`scripts/check_capability_profiles.py`) statically analyzes the codebase to detect capability usage (e.g., `std::net::TcpStream` implies `cap:network:connect`, `std::fs::write` implies `cap:fs:write`). If a subsystem uses a capability not declared in its profile, the gate fails with a specific diagnostic.

4. **Runtime capability guards.** Implement a `CapabilityGuard` middleware that wraps capability-gated operations. At runtime, the guard checks the calling subsystem's profile before allowing the operation. Violations are logged as security events and the operation is denied.

5. **Capability audit trail.** Every capability exercise is logged with subsystem identity, capability name, timestamp, and outcome (granted/denied). Audit logs feed into the telemetry namespace (10.13) for monitoring.

6. **Profile review workflow.** When a subsystem's capability profile changes (new capability added), the change requires explicit review. The CI gate detects profile diffs and flags them for security review.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Capability taxonomy documented in `docs/specs/section_10_11/bd-cvt_contract.md` with all capabilities enumerated.
2. At least 5 subsystem capability profiles exist in `capabilities/` directory.
3. `scripts/check_capability_profiles.py` with `--json` flag detects undeclared capability usage with zero false negatives on test fixtures.
4. `CapabilityGuard` implemented in `crates/franken-node/src/connector/capability_guard.rs` with grant/deny logging.
5. Audit trail events are emitted for every capability exercise in structured log format.
6. CI workflow includes capability profile gate as a required check.
7. Verification evidence written to `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-cvt/`.

## Key Dependencies

- 10.13 telemetry namespace for audit event schema.
- 10.13 stable error namespace for denied-capability error codes.
- Rust static analysis tooling (clippy custom lints or `cargo-audit` extensions).
- CI workflow infrastructure from 10.1.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests in `tests/test_check_capability_profiles.py` covering: profile parsing, static analysis detection, guard grant/deny logic.
- Integration test with a mock subsystem that attempts undeclared capability usage and confirms denial.
- Self-test mode validates the script correctly identifies a deliberately undeclared capability.
- Structured logging: `capability.granted`, `capability.denied`, `capability.profile_changed`, `capability.audit_gap` events.

## Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/section_10_11/bd-cvt_contract.md` — specification document.
- `capabilities/` directory with subsystem profiles.
- `crates/franken-node/src/connector/capability_guard.rs` — runtime guard.
- `scripts/check_capability_profiles.py` — verification script.
- `tests/test_check_capability_profiles.py` — unit tests.
- `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-cvt/verification_evidence.json` — evidence.
- `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-cvt/verification_summary.md` — summary.","acceptance_criteria":"AC for bd-cvt:
1. Every product subsystem declares a CapabilityProfile enum listing exactly the capabilities it requires (e.g., FileRead, NetConnect, CryptoSign); profiles are defined in a central registry module.
2. A compile-time or init-time narrowing gate enforces that a subsystem cannot acquire capabilities outside its declared profile; attempts to exceed the profile panic or return Err with a stable CAPABILITY_VIOLATION error code.
3. Profile narrowing is monotonic: once a subsystem drops a capability, it cannot re-acquire it within the same process lifetime.
4. A deny-by-default policy applies: subsystems start with an empty capability set and explicitly request each capability via a typed CxHandle (capability-context-first API pattern from Section 9G).
5. The capability registry exposes a machine-readable JSON manifest listing every subsystem and its granted capability set, suitable for offline audit.
6. Unit tests verify: (a) subsystem within profile succeeds, (b) subsystem exceeding profile is rejected, (c) monotonic narrowing prevents re-acquisition, (d) empty-profile subsystem cannot perform any privileged operation.
7. Integration test demonstrates two subsystems with disjoint profiles running concurrently without cross-contamination.
8. Structured log events use stable code CAPABILITY_GRANT / CAPABILITY_DENY / CAPABILITY_NARROW with trace correlation IDs.","notes":"Plan-space QA addendum (2026-02-20):
1) Preserve full canonical-plan scope; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
2) Completion requires comprehensive verification coverage appropriate to scope: unit tests, integration tests, and E2E scripts/workflows with detailed structured logging (stable event/error codes + trace correlation IDs).
3) Completion requires reproducible evidence artifacts (machine-readable + human-readable) that allow independent replay and root-cause triage.
4) Any implementation PR for this bead must include explicit links to the above test/logging artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:49.664148019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:30:01.850770607Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T03:30:01.850741122Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-11","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cvt","depends_on_id":"bd-3qo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:31.606158050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cvt","depends_on_id":"bd-5rh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:31.751296865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cvt","depends_on_id":"bd-cda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:46:31.811317014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
